title: Finding
description: One unit of diagnosis. A conforming diagnosis is an ordered list of 2-3 Findings. additionalProperties is intentionally true: a domain tool MAY carry extra first-class fields (e.g. integration-watcher's cohort prevalence, agent-researcher's scenario metadata) without losing conformance. The eight named fields are the closed required/conditional core; everything else is a domain extension point. See SPEC.md for the reference reading; this schema is normative.

id.description: Stable identifier within the diagnosis. Convention: single-letter origin prefix + 1-based index (H1, F1, D2).

title.description: One line naming the specific theory/pattern, with any '(Layer N)' annotation lifted into 'layer' and stripped.

layer.description: Exactly one causal layer. 1=measurement instrument, 2=machine-readable interface, 3=context at decision time, 4=sequence/architecture.

claim.description: 1-2 sentences stating the mechanism that produces the unwanted behavior, not that it occurs.

evidence.description: At least one verifiable evidence item. Tools MAY require more for their domain; the spec floor is one.

applyable.description: Whether the proposed change is expressible as a sequence of in-place structured edits. Layer does NOT determine this.

structured_edits.description: Required and non-empty iff applyable is true.

reason.description: Required and non-empty iff applyable is false. Why the change cannot be expressed as in-place edits.

verification.description: Falsifiable prediction: which measured quantity moves, by how much, and/or what would prove the Finding wrong.

allOf.description: D3: applyable true requires a non-empty structured_edits array.
D3: applyable false requires a non-empty reason string.

evidenceItem.description: Tagged union of five evidence variants, discriminated by 'type'. Additional properties are permitted as forward-compatible extension points (e.g. confidence, notes); they are NOT part of v0.1 and MUST NOT be relied on for conformance.
